_comment: DocAnalyser Help Texts - Edit this file to change help popup content
_instructions: Each entry has: title, description, and tips (list). Save and restart app to see changes.

_app_overview.title: DocAnalyser Intro
_app_overview.content:
DocAnalyser  —  Universal Document Analysis Tool

DocAnalyser helps you extract text from various sources and analyse
them using AI. It's designed to make document analysis quick and easy.

## 📥 What It Can Process

🎬 YouTube Videos
   • Automatically fetches transcripts/subtitles
   • Falls back to audio transcription if no subtitles available
   • Can download videos for offline processing

📄 Documents
   • PDF files (with OCR for scanned documents)
   • Word documents (.docx)
   • Text files (.txt, .rtf)
   • Drag-and-drop supported!

🎤 Audio & Video Files
   • MP3, WAV, M4A, MP4, and more
   • Multiple transcription engines available
   • Speaker identification and audio-linked summaries

🌐 Web Pages
   • Extracts readable text from any URL
   • Great for news articles and blog posts

📻 Podcasts & Substack
   • Apple Podcasts and RSS feeds fetched directly
   • Substack articles and podcast episodes

## 🤖 AI Analysis

Send your documents to AI for analysis using customisable prompts:

  • Summarise lengthy documents
  • Extract key points and themes
  • Answer questions about content
  • Translate or reformat text
  • Generate reports and analyses

Supported AI Providers:
  OpenAI (ChatGPT)  —  GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, and newer models
  Anthropic (Claude)
  Google (Gemini)  —  free tier available
  xAI (Grok)
  DeepSeek  —  very cost-effective
  Ollama  —  free local models (see Local AI Guide)

## 💡 Key Features

📚 Document Library
   All processed documents are automatically saved for later access.
   Search by title or content. Reload and export your documents anytime.

📦 Bulk Import
   Import multiple files or URLs at once. Perfect for batch processing
   YouTube playlists or document sets.

📎 Multi-Document Analysis
   Attach multiple documents from your library to a single prompt:
   • Consolidated summaries across multiple sources
   • Compare-and-contrast analyses
   • Gap analyses and cross-referencing
   • Thematic extraction across document sets

💬 Conversation Threading
   Ask follow-up questions — the AI remembers your conversation context,
   allowing you to drill deeper into topics.

📋 Prompt Library
   Save and reuse your favourite analysis prompts. Organise them into
   folders and share them with colleagues.

🔄 Import & Export
   Export documents and prompts to share with colleagues or keep as
   backups. Import shared prompt libraries or document sets into your
   own installation.

💾 Multiple Export Formats
   Save results as .txt, .docx, .rtf, or .pdf files.

## 🚀 Quick Start

Single Document:
  1. Enter a URL or drag-drop a file into the Source Input
  2. Click Load to extract the text
  3. Choose a prompt from the dropdown (or write your own)
  4. Select your AI provider and model
  5. Click Run Prompt to analyse!

Multiple Documents:
  1. Use Bulk Import to load multiple files/URLs at once
  2. Or load documents one at a time (they're saved to Library)
  3. Use the Attachments panel to select documents from Library
  4. Choose a prompt and click Run Prompt to analyse all together

## 🔒 Privacy: Local AI Option

Want to keep your documents completely private?

Ollama lets you run AI on your own computer — free and offline.
Your documents never leave your machine.

Open the Help menu and click 'Local AI Guide' for setup instructions.

Requires: 8 GB RAM minimum, works best with 16 GB or more.

Tip: Press F1 over any button for context-sensitive help!

universal_input.title: 📥 How to Load Files/URLs
universal_input.description:
LOADING A SINGLE FILE OR URL:
1️⃣ DRAG-AND-DROP
   • Drag a URL from your browser's address bar
   • Drag a file from File Explorer (Win+E)
   • Then click Load button

2️⃣ PASTE
   • Copy a file path or URL to clipboard
   • Click here and press Ctrl+V, then click Load button
   Tip: In File Explorer, right-click file → 'Copy as path'

3️⃣ BROWSE BUTTON
   • 'Open Explorer' - Opens File Explorer 
   • 'Web URL' - Opens browser
   • 'Documents Library' - Opens Documents Library

4️⃣ TYPE MANUALLY
   • Type a URL or file path
   • Click Load button

LOADING MULTIPLE FILES OR URLs:
   • Enter multiple file paths/URLs (one per line)
   • Click Load to add them to Documents Library and AI prompt

FROM DOCUMENTS LIBRARY:
   • Click 'Documents Library' button
   • Select documents and click '📎 Add to Prompt'

SUPPORTED CONTENT:
   • Documents: PDF, DOCX, TXT, RTF, HTML
   • Spreadsheets: XLSX, XLS, CSV
   • Audio/Video: MP3, MP4, WAV, M4A
   • Images: PNG, JPG (for OCR)
   • Web: YouTube, Substack, web pages

[LINK:supported_formats|→ See full list of supported file formats]

Loaded content is saved automatically to Documents Library.

load_button.title: ▶ Load
load_button.description:
Extract or transcribe text from the selected file or URL.

Text that is loaded is saved to the Documents Library.

bulk_button.title: 📦 Bulk Import
bulk_button.description:
Import multiple files and/or URLs into the Documents Library at the same time.

From there, use the 'Add Files' button below to select them (as well as other items in the Documents Library) so that they can be analysed as a group.

Possible applications include obtaining consolidated summaries, compare-and-contrast analyses or gap analyses.

There may be limits on your ability to do group analyses using local AI. For further information, consult the Local AI Guide, which can be accessed via the Help menu located in the top left corner. You can get an assessment of your computer's ability to use local AI by clicking the 'Settings' button below and then selecting 'System Check'.

force_reocr_button.title: 🔄 Force Re-OCR
force_reocr_button.description: Reprocess the current PDF with OCR (Optical Character Recognition). Use this if the initial text extraction missed content or if you've changed OCR settings to get a better result.

ocr_settings_button.title: ⚙️ OCR Settings
ocr_settings_button.description:
Configure how scanned PDFs are processed. Adjust language and quality settings.

Higher quality settings are slower more accurate.

Use 'Test OCR' to verify your setup works.

transcribe_audio_button.title: 🎤 Transcribe Audio
transcribe_audio_button.description:
Convert audio or video files to text using speech-to-text. Supports MP3, WAV, M4A, MP4, and other formats.

Choose your transcription engine in Audio Settings.

Faster-whisper runs locally and is free. The first transaction will be slower as the program is downloaded on first use and it is a big file.

You will need an API key if you select OpenAI Whisper or AssemblyAI. It is fast, accurate and has a small usage charge.

audio_settings_button.title: ⚙️ Audio Settings
audio_settings_button.description:
Configure audio transcription options including engine selection, language, speaker identification, and model quality.

Language will be auto-detected if you do not specify a language.

Speaker diarization identifies who's speaking. It's available in the cloud through AssemblyAI, but not locally through Faster-Whisper.

bypass_cache_checkbox.title: ☑️ Bypass Cache
bypass_cache_checkbox.description:
When checked, forces re-transcription even if this audio has been processed before. Normally, cached transcripts are reused, which is much faster than repeatedly transcribing.

Use this if you are changing transcription settings in search of a better quality result.

download_video_button.title: 📥 Download Video
download_video_button.description:
Download the YouTube video file to your computer. Useful for offline viewing or extracting audio for transcription.

The downloads are saved to your default Downloads folder.

Not implemented yet - will feature in next version.

yt_fallback_checkbox.title: ☑️ Audio Fallback
yt_fallback_checkbox.description:
When checked, if no official transcript is available, the app will download the audio and transcribe it using speech-to-text.

Useful for videos without subtitles.

Uses your selected transcription engine.

May incur API costs if using OpenAI Whisper.

reload_document_button.title: 📄 Reload Document
reload_document_button.description: Reload the current document from its original source. Useful if the source file has been updated.
tips: Works for local files, YouTube, and web pages
Clears any cached content
Conversation thread is preserved

export_document_button.title: 📝 Export As...
export_document_button.description: Save the current document content to a file. Choose from multiple formats including Word, PDF, and plain text.
tips: Available formats: .txt, .docx, .rtf, .pdf
Includes document metadata in exports
PDF export requires reportlab library

preview_area.title: 📄 Preview
preview_area.description:
Shows a preview of the loaded document or the latest AI response to a prompt you have run.

 Click 'Full Text' to view the full contents of the Preview screen.

full_text_button.title: 🔍 Full Text
full_text_button.description:
Opens the document in a new window.

Text that has been produced in response to an AI prompt that you have run can be edited in the Full Text window when they are loaded from the Documents Library. They are labelled [Response] in the Documents Library.

Source documents (that is, those which are loaded but which haven't been produced in response to an AI prompt) are read-only.

prompt_dropdown.title: 📋 Prompt Selector
prompt_dropdown.description:
Displays the name of a prompt stored in the Prompts Library. Saves retyping frequently used prompts.

The full text of the prompt appears in the box below.

Prompts can be added, edited or deleted in the Prompts Library. To access the Prompts Library, press the 'Prompts Library' button.

prompt_text.title: ✏️ Prompt Text
prompt_text.description:
This is the instruction you send to the AI along with the text(s) that you have loaded and/or attached. The AI sees both the prompt and the loaded/attached text(s).

You can edit prompts drawn from the Prompts Library here. But go to the Prompts Library if you want to save your edits.

You can write any prompt you wish here - you're not limited to prompts stored in the Prompts Library.The only purpose of the Prompts Library is to save time and keystrokes by allowing re-use of frequently-used prompts.

Be specific about what you are asking the AI to do.

run_prompt_button.title: ▶ Run Prompt
run_prompt_button.description:
Send your prompt and the text(s) to which it relates  to the selected AI model for processing.

The AI response is saved automatically to the Documents Library and appears in the Preview box below when it has been completed.

The AI response can be viewed in full by pressing the 'Full Text' button.

run_prompt_dropdown.title: ▼ Run Options
run_prompt_dropdown.description:
There are three options for running your prompt:

▶ Via DocAnalyser - Send to the selected cloud AI provider while staying within DocAnalyser.

🌐 Via Web - Run the prompt on the AI provider's website. The prompt and the text(s) it relates to are copied automatically to the clipboard. The selected cloud AI provider's website is opened automatically. You paste the clipboard contents into the AI provider's web interface and run the prompt. Optionally, 'Capture Response' can be used when the prompt response is complete to save it to the Documents Library, from where it will be accessible by selecting 'View Thread or 'Full Text'.

💻 Via Local AI - Run the prompt on your own computer. Requires LM Studio to be installed and running, with a local AI model loaded.

Using 'Via DocAnalyser' enables prompts to be run and stored with one click. Users need to obtain an API key from the AI provider in order to use it. It usually incurs some (small) costs. Press the 'AI Costs' button for further information.

'Via Local AI' is slower than the other options. It is free. It is a good option when privacy is a primary concern.

'Via Web' is free, fast and does not require an API key. While not difficult to use, it requires a few more clicks than the 'Via DocAnalyser' option.

followup_button.title: 💬 Follow-up
followup_button.description:
Ask follow-up question(s). Responses are saved in a 'conversation thread'. You can pick up and continue conversations in future sessions.

Conversation threads can be viewed via the 'View Thread' button.

You can switch AI models mid-conversation if needed.

web_response_capture.title: 📋 Capture Web Response
web_response_capture.description:
After using 'Via Web', a blue banner appears at the bottom of the window. This allows you to capture the AI's response and save it to the Documents Library.

Workflow:
1. Run prompt via 'Via Web' (copies to clipboard, opens browser)
2. Paste into AI chat and get response
3. Copy the AI's response
4. Click 'Capture Response' in DocAnalyser
5. Response is saved to Documents Library

This lets you get the speed and free access of web chat while still building a searchable library of AI responses.
tips: The captured response includes the original prompt for context
Click 'Dismiss' if you don't need to save the response
Captured responses can be loaded and used for follow-up questions

view_thread_button.title: 💬 View Thread
view_thread_button.description: Open a window showing your conversation history with the AI about the submitted text(s), including all prompts and responses.

start_fresh_button.title: 🔄 Start Fresh
start_fresh_button.description: Clear the current conversation thread so that you can start a new dialogue. The text(s) is/are preserved and remain(s) loaded but the AI will have no memory of prior exchanges.

history_button.title: 📜 History
history_button.description: Show the AI processing history for all texts in the current session.

prompts_library_button.title: 📋 Prompts Library
prompts_library_button.description:
Open the Prompts Library, where you can browse, create new and edit your saved prompts.

Use the Prompts Library to create and store custom prompts or prompt templates to save retyping.

documents_library_button.title: 📚 Documents Library
documents_library_button.description:
The Documents Library stores all your texts, transcripts and AI responses. It is searchable by title or content.

 Double-clicking a source document or conversation thread opens it in the  Thread viewer. The viewer opens automatically after an AI prompt is run.  

Double-clicking other documents reloads them for further AI analysis.

cancel_button.title: 🛑 Cancel
cancel_button.description: Stop the current AI processing operation. Useful if a request is taking too long or you've changed your mind.
tips: Enabled only during processing
May take a moment to fully stop
Partial results may still be shown

settings_button.title: ⚙️ Settings
settings_button.description: Open the main settings window where you can configure how DocAnalyser connects to AI services, processes documents, and manages stored data.
tips: AI Configuration: Choose your default AI provider and select which of their AI models you want to use. Click 'Refresh Models' to fetch the latest available models.

API Key: In order to run prompts using the 'Via DocAnalyser' option you need to obtain and enter an API key for cloud AI providers. Each provider requires its own key, which you obtain from their website. You do not need an API key if you run prompts using the 'Via Web' or the 'Via Local AI' options.

**LM Studio**: In order to run AI on your own computer (free and private) you need to install a separate package (LM Studio).  Click 'Test Connection' to verify LM Studio is installed and/or running. Click 'System Check' to assess how suitable your PC is for running local AI.
**Prompts Library**: Create, edit, and organise your collection of reusable prompts.

**Chunk Settings**: Control how large documents are split into smaller pieces before sending to the AI. Smaller chunks work better with local models; larger chunks preserve more context.

DocAnalyser uses chunking when prompts are run through the 'Via DocAnalyser' and 'Via Local AI' options.
\nDelays are provided in submitting successive chunks to avoid overloading the AI, which can cause crashes.
**OCR Settings**: Configure text recognition for scanned PDFs and images. Adjust quality settings to balance speed vs accuracy.
**Audio Settings**: Choose your transcription engine (OpenAI Whisper, AssemblyAI, etc.) and configure language and quality options for converting speech to text.
**Consolidation Tool**: Combine multiple AI analyses into a single summary - useful when you've processed several related documents.
**Cache Manager**: View and clear cached data, including downloaded transcripts, OCR results, and processed documents.

provider_dropdown.title: 🤖 AI Provider
provider_dropdown.description:
Select which cloud AI service(s) to use.

Supported providers include OpenAI (ChatGPT), Anthropic (Claude), Google (Gemini) and DeepSeek.

Local AI models can be accessed by selecting 'Via Local AI' in the 'Run Prompt' dropdown.

Cloud services are free if you run prompts through the 'Via Web' option.

Running prompts through the 'Via DocAnalyser' option requires an API key and usually incurs some costs - but Google Gemini has a generous free tier.

model_dropdown.title: 🧠 AI Model
model_dropdown.description:
Choose a cloud AI model.

VISION-CAPABLE MODELS (for images & handwriting):

• OpenAI: gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4-turbo
• Anthropic: All Claude 3.x and newer models
• Google: All Gemini 1.5+ models
• xAI: grok-2-vision, grok-vision-beta

For OCR of handwritten documents or images, select a vision-capable model.

TIP: gpt-4o-mini offers fast, affordable vision processing.

ai_costs_button.title: 💰 AI Costs
ai_costs_button.description: View pricing information for different AI providers and models. Relevant if you run prompts using the 'Via DocAnalyser' option.

library_search.title: 🔍 Search Documents
library_search.description: Search through saved documents by title or content. Results update as you type.

library_search_mode.title: 🔍 Search Mode
library_search_mode.description: Choose whether to search document titles only, full content, or both.
tips: Title: Fast, searches names only.
Content: Searches inside documents.
Both: Most comprehensive search.
Semantic Search: Search by meaning rather than literal words or phrases.

library_delete_button.title: 🗑️ Delete Selected
library_delete_button.description: Permanently delete the selected document from the Documents Library. This cannot be undone.
tips: Select a document first.
Confirmation required before deletion.
Associated thread data is also removed.

library_document_list.title: 📄 Document List
library_document_list.description: Your saved documents. Double-click to load a document into the main window for analysis.
tips: 💬 icon = document has a saved conversation thread
📄 = local file, 🎬 = YouTube, 🌐 = web page
[Source] = original content, [Product] = AI output
Sort by clicking column headers

library_preview.title: 👁️ Document Preview
library_preview.description: Shows a preview of the selected document's content.
tips: Click a document in the list to preview
Double-click to load it fully
Scroll to see more content

thread_copy_button.title: 📋 Copy
thread_copy_button.description:
Copy conversation content to clipboard.

Click to open a dialog where you can choose:

• All Exchanges - Copy all questions and AI responses

• Expanded Only - Copy only the exchanges you've expanded (collapsed ones are omitted)

• Complete: Source + Thread - Copy the source document AND all conversation exchanges

• Selection - Copy the currently selected text (select text first to enable)

Then select your preferred format (Plain Text or Formatted for Word/Email).
tips: Use 'All Exchanges' to copy just the conversation
Use 'Expanded Only' to copy selected exchanges
Use 'Complete' to share full context including the source
Formatted copies paste nicely into Word and Gmail

thread_archive_button.title: 📚 Archive to Library
thread_archive_button.description:
Save this conversation thread as a separate document in your Documents Library.

This creates a new [Thread] entry that you can reload later, separate from the original source document.

Useful for preserving important conversations or analyses that you want to reference later.

thread_clear_button.title: 🗑️ Clear Thread
thread_clear_button.description: Delete all messages in the current conversation thread. The document remains loaded.
tips: Cannot be undone
Frees up memory for long sessions
Document content is preserved

thread_save_button.title: 💾 Save As
thread_save_button.description:
Save conversation content to a file.

Click to open the Save As dialog where you can choose:

• Thread - Your questions and AI responses only

• Expanded Only - Only the exchanges you've expanded (collapsed ones are omitted)

• Complete - Source document AND all conversation exchanges in one file

Then select your preferred format (.docx, .txt, .rtf, or .pdf).
tips: Use 'Thread' for just the conversation
Use 'Expanded Only' to export selected exchanges
Use 'Complete' to share full context including the source
All formats available for all content types

settings_refresh_models.title: 🔄 Refresh Models
settings_refresh_models.description:
Fetch the latest available models from your selected AI provider's API.

Use this after entering a new API key or if models seem outdated.

settings_chunk.title: 📄 Chunk Settings
settings_chunk.description:
Configure how large documents are split into smaller chunks for AI processing.

Smaller chunks use less memory but may lose context. Larger chunks provide better context but require more resources.

settings_consolidation.title: 🔗 Consolidation Tool
settings_consolidation.description: Combine multiple document analyses into a single summary. Useful after bulk processing.
tips: Works with bulk processing results
Creates unified summary
Saves as new product document

settings_cache_manager.title: 🗃️ Cache Manager
settings_cache_manager.description:
View and manage cached data such as transcripts, web content, and processed documents.

Clearing the cache can free up disk space but will require re-processing of previously cached content.

cache_clear_ocr.title: 🗑️ Clear OCR Cache
cache_clear_ocr.description: Remove all cached OCR (text recognition) results. PDFs will be re-processed next time.
tips: Frees disk space
Use if OCR settings changed
Re-OCR will be slower initially

cache_clear_audio.title: 🗑️ Clear Audio Cache
cache_clear_audio.description: Remove all cached audio transcriptions. Audio files will be re-transcribed next time.
tips: Frees disk space
Use if transcription settings changed
May incur API costs to re-transcribe

cache_clear_outputs.title: 🗑️ Clear Outputs Cache
cache_clear_outputs.description: Remove all temporary output files created during processing.
tips: Safe to clear regularly
Does not affect saved documents
Frees up disk space

cache_clear_all.title: 🗑️ Clear All Cache
cache_clear_all.description: Remove all cached data at once - OCR, audio transcriptions, and temporary outputs.
tips: Maximum disk space recovery
All processing starts fresh
Confirmation required

audio_engine.title: 🎙️ Transcription Engine
audio_engine.description: Choose which speech-to-text service to use for audio transcription.
tips: OpenAI Whisper: Best accuracy, requires API key
faster-whisper: Free, runs locally
AssemblyAI: Good accuracy, speaker ID support

audio_language.title: 🌐 Language
audio_language.description: Specify the language of the audio, or leave blank for automatic detection.
tips: Auto-detect works well for most content
Specify language for better accuracy
Supports 50+ languages

audio_speaker_id.title: 👥 Speaker Diarization
audio_speaker_id.description: When enabled, the transcription will identify and label different speakers in the audio.
tips: Useful for interviews and meetings
May slightly slow processing
Not all engines support this

ocr_language.title: 🌐 OCR Language
ocr_language.description: Set the language for OCR text recognition. Use the correct language for better accuracy.
tips: English is the default
Install additional language packs if needed
Multiple languages can slow processing

ocr_quality.title: 📊 OCR Quality
ocr_quality.description: Choose the quality preset for OCR processing. Higher quality is more accurate but slower.
tips: Fast: Quick but less accurate
Balanced: Good for most documents
Best: Highest accuracy, slowest

ocr_test.title: 🧪 Test OCR
ocr_test.description: Test your OCR setup to verify Tesseract is correctly installed and configured.
tips: Shows if Tesseract is found
Displays installed languages
Helps diagnose problems

prompts_list.title: 📋 Prompts List
prompts_list.description: Your saved prompt templates. Click to select, double-click to use immediately.
tips: Prompts are saved automatically
Use 'New' to create custom prompts
Edit button modifies selected prompt

prompts_new_button.title: ➕ New Prompt
prompts_new_button.description: Create a new prompt template. Enter a name and the prompt text you want to save.
tips: Give prompts descriptive names
Prompts can include instructions for any analysis task
New prompts appear in the dropdown on the main window

prompts_edit_button.title: ✏️ Edit Prompt
prompts_edit_button.description: Modify the selected prompt's name or text.
tips: Select a prompt first
Changes are saved automatically
Can't edit while prompt is in use

prompts_duplicate_button.title: 📄 Duplicate Prompt
prompts_duplicate_button.description: Create a copy of the selected prompt. Useful for making variations of existing prompts.
tips: Copy is named 'Original Name (copy)'
Edit the copy to customise it
Great for creating prompt variations

prompts_delete_button.title: 🗑️ Delete Prompt
prompts_delete_button.description: Permanently remove the selected prompt from your library.
tips: Cannot be undone
Built-in prompts can be restored
Confirmation required

prompts_use_button.title: ▶ Use Prompt
prompts_use_button.description: Load the selected prompt into the main window and close the library.
tips: Same as double-clicking a prompt
Prompt appears in the text area
You can edit it before running

settings_api_key.title: 🔑 API Key
settings_api_key.description:
You need a valid API key if you choose to run prompts through the 'Via DocAnalyser' option.

You do not need an API key to run prompts through the 'Via Web' or 'Via Local AI' options.
tips: Click the link below to get an API key for your selected provider
API keys are stored securely in your local config file
Each AI provider requires its own API key

settings_lm_studio_url.title: 🖥️ LM Studio URL
settings_lm_studio_url.description: The address where LM Studio is running. Default is http://localhost:1234 for local use.
tips: LM Studio must be running first
Use default unless running remotely
Test connection with Refresh Models

settings_save_button.title: 💾 Save All Settings
settings_save_button.description: Save all your current settings to disk. Settings persist between sessions.
tips: Settings auto-save on close too
Includes API keys and preferences
Stored in your user data folder

semantic_search_button.title: 🧠 Test Semantic Search
semantic_search_button.description: Test the semantic search capability which finds documents by meaning rather than exact keywords.
tips: Requires embeddings to be generated
More intelligent than keyword search
Experimental feature

lm_studio_setup.title: 🖥️ Running AI Locally with LM Studio
lm_studio_setup.description: LM Studio lets you run AI models on your own computer - completely free and private. Your documents never leave your machine. This is ideal for users who want privacy, have no internet, or prefer not to pay for cloud AI.
tips: **What You Need**: A PC with at least 16GB of RAM (32GB recommended for larger models). A dedicated graphics card (GPU) helps but isn't essential.
**Step 1 - Download LM Studio**: Visit lmstudio.ai and download the free application for Windows, Mac, or Linux. Install it like any other program.

**Step 2 - Download a Model**: Open LM Studio, go to the 'Discover' tab, and search for a model. For simple summaries, we recommend: Phi-3-mini (3.8B) - fast and efficient, Llama 3.2 3B - good all-rounder, or Mistral 7B - higher quality but slower.
**Step 3 - Load the Model**: Go to the 'Local Server' tab (left sidebar), select your downloaded model, and click 'Start Server'. Wait until it says 'Server running'.

**Step 4 - Connect DocAnalyser**: In DocAnalyser, go to Settings, select 'LM Studio (Local)' as your provider, and click 'Test Connection'. If it works, your local models will appear in the model dropdown.
**Choosing a Model Size**: Smaller models (3B parameters) are faster but less nuanced. Larger models (7B-13B) give better quality but are slower. For basic summaries, 3B models work very well.
**Chunk Size Tip**: When using local models, go to Settings > Chunk Settings and select 'Tiny' or 'Small'. This prevents overloading the model with too much text at once.

**Speed Expectations**: Local AI is slower than cloud APIs. A 500-word summary might take 15-45 seconds with a 3B model, compared to 5-10 seconds with a cloud API. The trade-off is complete privacy and zero cost.

local_model_recommendations.title: 📊 Recommended Local Models
local_model_recommendations.description: If you're new to local AI, here are the best models to start with based on your computer's capabilities and what you want to do.
tips: **For 16GB RAM - Basic Summaries**: Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct (3.8B) - Microsoft's efficient model, great for summaries and Q&A. Very fast.
**For 16GB RAM - Better Quality**: Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct - Meta's latest small model. Good balance of speed and quality.
**For 32GB RAM - Higher Quality**: Mistral-7B-Instruct - Excellent quality, handles complex analysis well. Slower but worth it for important documents.
**For 32GB+ RAM - Best Quality**: Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct or Phi-4 (14B) - Near cloud-quality results, but requires patience.
**Avoid for Summarisation**: Models over 13B parameters unless you have 64GB+ RAM and a good GPU. They'll be frustratingly slow.
**Look for 'Instruct' or 'Chat' versions**: These are tuned to follow instructions. Base models without these labels won't work as well for document analysis.
**Quantised Models**: In LM Studio, you'll see options like Q4_K_M or Q8. Lower numbers (Q4) use less RAM but slightly lower quality. Q4_K_M is usually the sweet spot.

attachment_add_files.title: Add Files
attachment_add_files.description:
Attach files from your computer to include in the AI prompt.

Supported formats: TXT, PDF, DOCX, DOC, RTF, MD, CSV, JSON.

The text content will be extracted and sent along with your prompt to the AI.

attachment_from_library.title: From Library
attachment_from_library.description:
Attach documents from the Documents Library.

This lets you include previously fetched YouTube transcripts, web pages, audio transcripts or other documents as context for your AI prompt.

attachment_remove.title: Remove
attachment_remove.description:
Remove the selected attachment(s) from the list.

Select one or more items in the list below, then click this button. You can also press the Delete key.

attachment_clear.title: Clear
attachment_clear.description: Remove all attachments from the list.

attachment_listbox.title: Attachments List
attachment_listbox.description:
Shows files attached to your prompt.

Each attachment displays its filename and word count. The total token estimate helps you stay within AI context limits.

Select items here to remove them with the Remove button or Delete key.

library_embeddings_button.title: 🧠 Embeddings Manager
library_embeddings_button.description:
Opens the Semantic Search Embedding Manager.

Semantic search lets you find documents by meaning rather than exact keywords. For example, searching for 'climate change' can find documents about 'global warming' even if those exact words aren't used.

For this to work, documents need 'embeddings' - numerical representations of their meaning that AI can compare. This manager shows which documents have embeddings and lets you generate them.

Generating embeddings uses the OpenAI API and costs approximately $0.0001 per document (very cheap).

library_add_to_prompt_button.title: 📎 Add to Prompt
library_add_to_prompt_button.description:
Add selected documents as attachments for multi-document analysis.

This lets you analyze multiple library documents together with a single prompt. For example:
• Compare and contrast different documents
• Summarize multiple related articles
• Extract common themes across several sources

Workflow:
1. Select one or more documents in the library tree
2. Click 'Add to Prompt'
3. The documents are added as attachments
4. Library closes automatically
5. Select a prompt and click 'Run' to analyze all documents together

The attachments use the already-extracted text from the library - no re-processing needed.
tips: Select multiple documents using Ctrl+click or Shift+click
Works with any library document (PDFs, transcripts, web pages, etc.)
Attachments are shown in the status bar with word count
Use 'Add Files/URLs' button in main window to manage attachments

add_sources_button.title: ➕ Add Files/URLs
add_sources_button.description:
Import files/URLs from your computer or network, the Web or the Documents Library. They can then be referenced by your AI prompt - eg 'Provide a one-paragraph summary of each of the attached texts' or 'Identify the key points of agreement and disagreement in the views expressed in the attached texts'.

You can choose to:
• save imported files/URLs to the Documents Library (for future reference), and/or
• include them in the current prompt (eg for a multi-document analysis that you don't need to keep).

sources_drop_entry.title: 📥 Drop/Paste Input
sources_drop_entry.description:
Drop files or URLs here, paste with Ctrl+V, or type manually and press Enter.

Accepts:
• File paths (drag from Explorer or type full path)
• Web URLs (drag from browser address bar or paste)
• YouTube URLs
• Multiple items separated by newlines

Items are added to the list below. Click 'Fetch' to process them.

sources_add_entry_button.title: + Add
sources_add_entry_button.description:
Add the URL or file path from the input field to the list below.

Same as pressing Enter in the input field.

sources_listbox.title: 📋 Sources List
sources_listbox.description:
Shows the URLs, files, and library documents queued for processing.

Select items and press Delete to remove them, or use the Remove button.

Double-click an item to edit it (not yet implemented).

sources_add_files_button.title: 📁 Add Files...
sources_add_files_button.description:
Browse your computer to select files to add.

Supported formats: PDF, DOCX, DOC, TXT, MD, HTML, RTF, CSV, JSON, and URL shortcut files (.url).

You can select multiple files at once using Ctrl+click or Shift+click.

sources_add_url_button.title: 🔗 Add URL...
sources_add_url_button.description:
Manually enter a URL or file path to add to the list.

Useful when you want to type or paste a URL without using the main input field.

sources_from_library_button.title: 📚 From Library...
sources_from_library_button.description:
Select documents from your Documents Library to include.

This lets you re-use previously processed documents without fetching them again.

Useful for combining existing library documents with new sources.

sources_fetch_button.title: 📥 Fetch
sources_fetch_button.description:
Process all items in the list and save them to the selected destination.

If 'Save to Documents Library' is selected:
• Items are fetched and permanently saved to your library
• Use the dropdown arrow for scheduling options

If 'Add to current prompt context' is selected:
• Items are fetched and temporarily added for the current analysis
• They will be cleared when you close the app or start a new document

sources_remove_button.title: 🗑️ Remove
sources_remove_button.description:
Remove the selected item(s) from the list.

Select one or more items in the list above, then click this button.

You can also press the Delete or Backspace key.

sources_clear_button.title: 🧹 Clear
sources_clear_button.description:
Remove all items from the list.

This clears the queue without processing anything.

sources_cancel_button.title: Cancel
sources_cancel_button.description:
Stop the current processing operation.

Items that have already been processed will be kept; remaining items will not be processed.

sources_destination_library.title: 📚 Save to Documents Library
sources_destination_library.description:
Fetched sources will be permanently saved to your Documents Library.

Use this option when you want to:
• Keep documents for future reference
• Build up a searchable library of content
• Process documents now and analyse them later

sources_destination_context.title: 📎 Add to current prompt context
sources_destination_context.description:
Fetched sources will be temporarily added to your current prompt context.

Use this option when you want to:
• Quickly analyse multiple documents together
• Include external content in a single AI prompt
• Perform one-off multi-document analysis

⚠️ Items will be cleared when you close the app or start a new document.

source_mode_single.title: ○ One source only
source_mode_single.description:
Select this when you want to load a single file or URL.

Shows the text input field where you can:
• Type or paste a URL or file path
• Drag and drop a file
• Use Browse to select a file

The loaded content will be saved to the Documents Library.

source_mode_multiple.title: ○ More than one source
source_mode_multiple.description:
Select this when you want to work with multiple files or URLs.

Shows the Add button which opens the 'Add Files/URLs' dialog where you can:
• Add multiple files and URLs at once
• Choose to save to Library or add to Prompt Context
• Process items in batch

source_mode_remember.title: ☐ Remember
source_mode_remember.description:
When ticked, your source mode selection will be remembered for future sessions.

Untick this to clear the saved preference and return to the default state (no mode selected) next time you start the app.

thread_selection_menu.title: 📋 Selection
thread_selection_menu.description:
Work with selected text in the conversation.

First, select some text by clicking and dragging, then click this button to open a dialog where you can choose:

• Copy to Clipboard - Copy the selected text (Plain Text or Formatted)

• Save to File - Save the selection as .docx, .txt, .rtf, or .pdf
tips: Select text first by clicking and dragging
Use Formatted copy for Word and Gmail
You can also press Ctrl+C to copy selected text directly
All four file formats are available for saving

thread_save_disk_menu.title: 💾 Save As
thread_save_disk_menu.description:
Save conversation content to a file.

Click to open the Save As dialog where you can choose:

• Thread - Your questions and AI responses only

• Expanded Only - Only the exchanges you've expanded (collapsed ones are omitted)

• Complete - Source document AND all conversation exchanges in one file

Then select your preferred format (.docx, .txt, .rtf, or .pdf).
tips: Use 'Thread' for just the conversation
Use 'Expanded Only' to export selected exchanges
Use 'Complete' to share full context including the source
All formats available for all content types

thread_end_conversation_button.title: 🛑 End Conversation
thread_end_conversation_button.description:
End the current conversation and close this window.

The conversation will be saved to the document before being cleared. The document remains loaded so you can start a new conversation with fresh questions.

Use this when you've finished exploring a topic and want to start over.

thread_close_button.title: Close
thread_close_button.description:
Close this window and return to the main application.

Your conversation is preserved - you can reopen it later using the View Thread button.

Keyboard shortcut: Escape

thread_followup_input.title: ❓ Follow-up Question
thread_followup_input.description:
Type a follow-up question here to continue the conversation with the AI.

The AI has context from the entire conversation thread, so you can ask clarifying questions, request more detail, or explore related topics.

Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+Enter to submit

thread_submit_button.title: 📤 Submit
thread_submit_button.description:
Send your follow-up question to the AI.

The AI will respond using the full conversation context, including the original document and all previous exchanges.

Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+Enter

thread_display_area.title: 💬 Conversation Thread
thread_display_area.description:
This area shows your conversation history with the AI about the current document.

The text is editable - you can click and edit to fix names, places, or other mistakes the AI made.

After editing, click 'Save Edits' or press Ctrl+S to save your changes.
tips: Ctrl+C to copy selected text
Ctrl+V to paste text
Ctrl+S to save edits
Ctrl+H or Ctrl+R to focus find/replace
Links are clickable - click to open in browser

thread_find_field.title: 🔍 Find
thread_find_field.description:
Enter the text you want to find (e.g., a misspelled name).

Search is case-insensitive.

Press Enter or click Find to locate the next occurrence.
tips: Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+H or Ctrl+R to focus this field
Press Enter to find next
Search wraps around at end of document

thread_replace_field.title: ✏️ Replace
thread_replace_field.description:
Enter the correct text to replace with.

Press Enter or click Replace to replace the current selection and find the next occurrence.

thread_find_button.title: 🔍 Find
thread_find_button.description:
Find the next occurrence of the search text.

The found text will be highlighted in yellow and selected.

Search wraps around to the beginning when reaching the end.

thread_replace_button.title: ↔️ Replace
thread_replace_button.description:
Replace the currently selected text (if it matches) and find the next occurrence.

If nothing is selected or the selection doesn't match, just finds the next occurrence.

thread_replace_all_button.title: 🔁 Replace All
thread_replace_all_button.description:
Replace ALL occurrences of the search text throughout the entire conversation.

The status will show how many replacements were made.

Remember to click 'Save Edits' to save your changes!

audio_engine_selection.title: 🎤 Transcription Engine
audio_engine_selection.description:
Choose which service to use for converting audio to text.

• faster-whisper (Local): Free, runs on your computer, no file size limit. Best for most users.

• OpenAI Whisper (Cloud): Fast and accurate. Costs ~$0.006/minute. 25MB file limit. Uses your OpenAI API key.

• AssemblyAI (Cloud): Excellent accuracy with speaker identification (who said what). Free tier: 100 hours/month.
tips: Start with faster-whisper - it's free!
Use AssemblyAI for interviews/podcasts needing speaker ID
OpenAI Whisper is fast but has a 25MB file limit

audio_api_keys.title: 🔑 API Keys
audio_api_keys.description:
Cloud transcription services require API keys.

• OpenAI: Same key used for GPT models. Get one at platform.openai.com/api-keys

• AssemblyAI: Free signup at assemblyai.com/dashboard/signup
  - Free tier: 100 hours/month
  - Supports speaker identification
tips: Click 'Get Key' to open the signup page
Keys are stored securely in your local config
You can use Show/Hide to verify your key

dictate_button.title: 🎙️ Dictate (Speech-to-Text)
dictate_button.description:
Record speech from your microphone and convert it to text.

Go to Settings/Audio Transcription Settings/Dictation (Speech-to-Text) to configure speech transcription options. Choose between: Local (free & private - audio stays on your computer); Cloud (faster & more accurate - sends audio to your selected cloud audio transcription engine); or Local first, cloud fallback (should local transcription fail).

The transcribed speech is loaded as a document, ready for AI analysis, and is saved automatically in the Documents Library.

dictation_dialog.title: 🎙️ Dictation Recording
dictation_dialog.description:
Record speech from your microphone and convert it to text.

You can record in multiple segments - useful for longer recordings where you need to pause and continue later.

Workflow:
1. Click 'Record' to start recording
2. Click 'Stop' when you need a break
3. Click 'Continue' to add more speech
4. Repeat steps 2-3 as needed
5. Click 'Transcribe' to combine all segments and convert to text

Use 'Start Fresh' to clear all segments and begin again.

The segment counter shows how many recordings you've made and the total duration across all segments.
tips: You can pause and resume as many times as needed
All segments are combined into one transcription
Total duration is shown across all segments
Use 'Start Fresh' to discard and start over

multi_page_ocr_button.title: 📑 Scan Pages (Multi-Page OCR)
multi_page_ocr_button.description:
Process multiple images as a single document.

Ideal for handwritten documents or scanned pages that are saved as separate image files.

IMPORTANT: Click this button FIRST, then add your images in the dialog that opens. Don't drag images onto the main input field - that processes them one at a time.

Workflow:
1. Click this 'Scan Pages' button
2. In the dialog, click 'Add Images' to select your page images
3. Reorder pages if needed using the up/down arrows
4. Click 'Process OCR' to extract text from all pages
5. The combined result is saved as a single document

Supports PNG, JPEG, TIFF, BMP, and GIF images.

Uses your current OCR settings (local Tesseract or Cloud AI).

multi_page_ocr_dialog.title: 📄 Multi-Page OCR Dialog
multi_page_ocr_dialog.description:
Process multiple images as pages of a single document.

This dialog lets you:
• Add multiple image files (scanned pages, photos of handwritten text)
• Reorder pages before processing
• Process all pages with your selected OCR method
• Get one combined document with page numbers preserved

Workflow:
1. Click 'Add Images' to select image files
2. Use ⬆️/⬇️ buttons to reorder if needed
3. Click 'Process OCR' to start
4. Progress is shown for each page
5. Result is saved to Documents Library

OCR Mode (configured in Settings):
• Local first: Uses Tesseract, falls back to Cloud AI if confidence is low
• Cloud direct: Sends images directly to your AI provider (best for handwriting)
tips: Files are sorted naturally when added (page1, page2, page10 not page1, page10, page2)
Use Cloud AI direct mode for handwritten documents
You can remove individual pages with the Remove button or Delete key
Progress shows which page is being processed
Cancel stops processing - pages already done are not saved

set_default_prompt_button.title: ⭐ Set as Default
set_default_prompt_button.description:
Make the selected prompt the default choice when DocAnalyser starts.

Instead of always starting with the first prompt in the list, DocAnalyser will pre-select your chosen prompt.

This saves time if you frequently use the same prompt.
tips: Select a prompt from the list first
The default is saved and persists across sessions
You can change the default at any time

settings_system_check.title: 💻 System Check
settings_system_check.description: Click to assess the suitability of your system for running local AI.

settings_test_connection.title: 🔌 Test Connection
settings_test_connection.description:
Test the connection to your local LM Studio server.

This verifies that LM Studio is running and the server is accessible at the configured URL.

settings_check_for_updates.title: 🔄 Check for Updates
settings_check_for_updates.description:
Check if a newer version of DocAnalyser is available.

If an update is found, you'll be given the option to download it.

settings_feature_status.title: 📋 Feature Status
settings_feature_status.description:
View the status of all DocAnalyser features and dependencies.

This shows which features are available, which require additional setup, and any issues detected.

settings_export_diagnostics.title: 💾 Export Diagnostics
settings_export_diagnostics.description:
Export diagnostic information about your DocAnalyser installation.

This creates a file containing system info, configuration, and feature status that can help troubleshoot issues.

settings_save_all.title: 💾 Save All Settings
settings_save_all.description:
Save all settings and close the Settings window.

Your preferences will be remembered for future sessions.

settings_close.title: ❌ Close
settings_close.description:
Close the Settings window without saving.

Any unsaved changes will be lost.

process_ocr_button.title: 📑 Process OCR
process_ocr_button.description:
Extract text from all the images/pages listed above.

The OCR method depends on the Text Type selection:

• Printed Text: Uses local Tesseract OCR (free, private, works offline). Best for typed documents, books, and printed materials.

• Handwriting: Uses Cloud AI vision (GPT-4o or similar). Requires an API key and internet connection. Much better at reading handwritten text, but incurs small API costs.

After processing, you'll see a preview where you can:
• Edit the text manually
• Use voice commands to make corrections
• Copy to clipboard or Save As file
• Accept & Save to Documents Library

Tip: For difficult handwriting, use the Context Hint field to provide names, dates, and topics that might appear in the document - this helps the AI make better guesses.
tips: Handwriting recognition requires Cloud AI (GPT-4o recommended)
Local Tesseract OCR is free but only works well on printed text
Processing time depends on number of pages and OCR method
You can cancel mid-process - partial results are not saved

ocr_text_type_dropdown.title: 📄 OCR Text Type Selection
ocr_text_type_dropdown.description:
Choose the best OCR method based on your document type AND image quality.

📸 IMAGE SOURCE MATTERS!
Flatbed Scanner (recommended):
• Local OCR works well if image quality is OK
• Online AI produces near-perfect results
Phone Photos:
• Local OCR struggles. Online AI is much better
🔤 PRINTED TEXT (Local OCR)
✅ Best for:
• Flatbed-scanned documents
• Clean, modern printed text
• Books, articles, typed documents
⚠️ Limitations:
• Struggles with phone photos
• Poor results on faded/old documents
• Cannot read handwriting
• But FREE! (runs locally)
• Privacy: Complete (nothing leaves your computer)
HANDWRITING/LOW-QUALITY PRINT (Online AI)
✅ Best for:
• Handwritten documents (letters, notes, forms)
• Phone photos of any document
• Old, faded, or poor-quality scans
• Documents with unusual fonts or layouts
⚠️ Limitations:
• SMALL COST (uses API credits)
• Requires API key from your AI provider

QUICK DECISION GUIDE
Flatbed scan + good quality printed text → Use 'Printed Text' (free & fast)
Flatbed scan + handwriting or poor quality printed text → Use 'Handwriting'
Phone photo of anything  → Use 'Handwriting'
Old/faded document - Use 'Handwriting'
CONFIDENCE THRESHOLD
When using 'Printed Text' mode, local OCR reports a confidence score.
If this falls below your threshold (set in OCR Settings), you'll be prompted to retry with Online AI. Adjust your threshold in: Settings → OCR Settings → OCR Processing Mode
Higher threshold (e.g. 90%) = More prompts to use AI, better quality
Lower threshold (e.g. 50%) = Fewer prompts, accept more local results

prompt_history_button.title: 📜 Version History
prompt_history_button.description:
View and manage all saved versions of this prompt.

Each time you edit and save a prompt, DocAnalyser creates a new version while preserving the previous ones. This lets you:

• See all past versions with timestamps
• View the full text of any version
• Restore an older version if you want to undo changes
• Track how your prompts have evolved over time

HOW TO USE:

1. Select a prompt from the tree
2. Click this '📜 History' button
3. A list of all versions appears:
   • Version 0 = Original version
   • Version 1, 2, 3... = Your edits in chronological order
   • ⭐ marks the currently active version
4. Select a version and click 'View' to see its full text
5. Click 'Restore' to make an old version current again
6. Click 'Close' to exit without changes

VERSION MANAGEMENT:

• Up to 10 versions are kept per prompt
• When the limit is reached, the oldest versions are automatically deleted
• The original/default version (for system prompts) is always preserved
• Restoring a version doesn't delete anything - it just changes which version is 'current'
• You can switch back and forth between versions as many times as you like

WHY USE VERSION HISTORY:

• Experiment freely - you can always go back
• Compare different phrasings to see what works best
• Recover from accidental changes
• Track improvements to your prompts over time

thread_undo_button.title: ↩️ Undo Edits
thread_undo_button.description:
Undo your last edit to the text.

You can click multiple times to undo further back. There's no limit to how many edits you can undo.

Keyboard shortcuts:
• Ctrl+Z - Undo
• Ctrl+Y - Redo

Edits are automatically saved when you close this window, so use Undo if you make changes you want to reverse before closing.
tips: Works like Undo in Microsoft Word
Ctrl+Z also works
Ctrl+Y to redo (restore undone changes)
Edits auto-save when you close the window

thread_new_branch_button.title: 🌿 New Branch
thread_new_branch_button.description:
Start a new conversation branch using the same source document.

This creates a fresh conversation thread that appears in the Branch dropdown at the top of this window. You can switch between branches to explore different lines of questioning.

Useful when you want to ask different questions about the same document without affecting your existing conversation.
tips: Each branch is a separate conversation thread
Use the Branch dropdown to switch between branches
All branches share the same source document
Branches are saved automatically

unified_viewer_mode_toggle.title: 📄/💬 View Mode Toggle
unified_viewer_mode_toggle.description:
Switch between viewing the source document (in the screen headed 'Source Document') and the conversation thread (in the screen headed 'Conversation Thread').

• 'View Source'shows the text of the original file or URL that was loaded. You can show or hide the source document when the header of the window is 'Conversation Thread'.
• 'View Thread' shows the original text plus all the following AI prompts and responses.

Both views are editable. Edits are saved automatically when you close the window.

unified_viewer_source_toggle.title: 👁️ Show/Hide Source
unified_viewer_source_toggle.description:
Show or hide the source document section in conversation view.

When shown, the source document appears in a collapsible section at the top of the conversation.

unified_viewer_exchanges_toggle.title: 📂/📁 Expand/Collapse All
unified_viewer_exchanges_toggle.description:
Show or hide the source document and all conversation exchanges at once.

• 'Expand All' shows the full content of the source document and every prompt and response
• 'Collapse All' shows only the exchange headers (timestamps and summaries)

Useful for navigating long conversations or getting an overview of the discussion.

prompt_tree_view.title: Prompt Library Tree
prompt_tree_view.description:
This tree shows all your prompts organised into folders.

Click a prompt to view its text in the panel on the right. Click a folder to expand or collapse it.

You can drag and drop prompts between folders to reorganise them.
tips: Right-click any item for a context menu with additional options
Double-click a prompt to start editing it immediately
Prompts marked with a star are favourites and appear at the top of the dropdown in the main window
Green text indicates the default prompt

prompt_text_editor.title: Prompt Text
prompt_text_editor.description:
This area shows the text of the currently selected prompt.

To edit: click the Edit button (or double-click here) to enter edit mode. The background turns pale yellow when you are editing. Press Save when finished, or Escape to discard changes.

Edits are saved as new versions, so you can always go back to a previous version using the History button.
tips: Ctrl+S saves while editing
Escape cancels the current edit
Each save creates a new version in the prompt's history

prompt_expand_all_button.title: Expand All
prompt_expand_all_button.description:
Opens every folder in the tree so you can see all prompts at once.

Useful for getting an overview of your entire prompt library.

prompt_collapse_all_button.title: Collapse All
prompt_collapse_all_button.description:
Closes every folder in the tree, showing only the top-level folder names.

Useful for tidying up the view when the tree gets crowded.

prompt_new_folder_button.title: New Folder
prompt_new_folder_button.description:
Create a new folder to organise your prompts.

If a folder is currently selected, the new folder is created inside it (as a subfolder). If nothing is selected, or if a prompt is selected, the new folder is created at the root level.
tips: Click in empty space below the tree to deselect everything before creating a root-level folder
Folders can be nested up to 4 levels deep
Drag and drop prompts into folders to organise them

prompt_new_prompt_button.title: New Prompt
prompt_new_prompt_button.description:
Create a new blank prompt.

The prompt is placed in the currently selected folder. If no folder is selected, it goes into the General folder.

After creating, you can type the prompt text in the editor on the right and click Save.
tips: Give your prompt a descriptive name so you can find it later
New prompts start in edit mode so you can begin typing immediately

prompt_rename_button.title: Rename
prompt_rename_button.description:
Rename the currently selected prompt or folder.

Select an item in the tree first, then click this button to change its name.
tips: You can also right-click an item and choose Rename
Prompt names must be unique within the same folder

prompt_delete_button.title: Delete
prompt_delete_button.description:
Delete the currently selected prompt or folder.

You will be asked to confirm before anything is deleted. Deleting a folder also deletes all prompts and subfolders inside it.

This action cannot be undone.
tips: System default prompts cannot be deleted, but you can modify them
If you accidentally delete a prompt, you may be able to recover it by importing from a backup .docanalyser file

prompt_move_up_button.title: Move Up
prompt_move_up_button.description:
Move the selected prompt or folder up one position within its current folder.

This controls the display order in the tree and in the prompt dropdown on the main window.

prompt_move_down_button.title: Move Down
prompt_move_down_button.description:
Move the selected prompt or folder down one position within its current folder.

This controls the display order in the tree and in the prompt dropdown on the main window.

prompt_edit_button.title: Edit Prompt
prompt_edit_button.description:
Enter edit mode for the currently selected prompt.

The text editor background changes to pale yellow to indicate you are in edit mode. Make your changes, then click Save to create a new version, or press Escape to discard.
tips: You can also double-click the prompt text to start editing
Ctrl+S saves your edit
Escape discards unsaved changes
Each save creates a new version — use History to view or restore previous versions

prompt_use_button.title: Use Prompt
prompt_use_button.description:
Copy this prompt into the main window's prompt box, ready to run against a loaded document.

This closes the Prompts Library and switches you back to the main window with this prompt selected.

prompt_save_button.title: Save Prompt
prompt_save_button.description:
Save the current edits to the prompt text.

This creates a new version of the prompt. You can view and restore previous versions using the History button.

Only available when you are in edit mode (yellow background).
tips: Ctrl+S also saves while in edit mode
The prompt keeps up to 20 versions of its history

prompt_favorite_button.title: Favourites
prompt_favorite_button.description:
Toggle whether this prompt is marked as a favourite.

Favourite prompts appear in a special section at the top of the prompt dropdown in the main window, making them quicker to access.

Click again to remove the favourite status.
tips: Favourites are shown with a star in the tree view
You can have as many favourites as you like

prompt_restore_default_button.title: Restore Default
prompt_restore_default_button.description:
Reset this system prompt back to its original default text.

This only applies to prompts that were included with DocAnalyser by default. If you have modified a default prompt and want the original back, use this button.

This does not affect user-created prompts.
tips: Your modified version is kept in the version history even after restoring
Only visible for system default prompts, not user-created ones

prompt_export_button.title: Export Prompts
prompt_export_button.description:
Export the currently highlighted item in the tree to a .docanalyser file that can be shared or backed up.

What gets exported depends on what you have selected:

• If a PROMPT is highlighted: that single prompt is exported (including all its version history).
• If a FOLDER is highlighted: the entire folder is exported, including all prompts inside it, any subfolders, and their contents.

If nothing is highlighted, you will be asked to select something first.
tips: Click a prompt or folder in the tree BEFORE clicking Export
To export multiple individual prompts, put them in a folder and export the folder
The .docanalyser file is a portable package that preserves folder structure and version history
You can also right-click any item for export options

prompt_import_button.title: Import Prompts
prompt_import_button.description:
Import prompts from a .docanalyser file.

Opens a file picker to select a .docanalyser file, then shows a preview dialog where you can:

• See all the prompts and folders contained in the file
• Tick or untick individual items to choose what to import
• Choose one or more destination folders (you can select multiple)
• Optionally recreate the original folder structure from the file
• Control how duplicate prompt names are handled
tips: You can import into multiple folders at once by ticking several destinations
'Recreate original folder structure' will create any folders from the source file that don't already exist
Use 'Rename' mode to keep both versions if a prompt name already exists
Imported prompts include their full version history

import_dialog_items.title: Select Items to Import
import_dialog_items.description:
This list shows all the prompts and folders contained in the .docanalyser file.

Use the checkboxes to select which items you want to import:

• Ticking a folder checkbox ticks or unticks all prompts inside it
• [+] indicates a folder, [P] indicates an individual prompt
• The grey text shows a preview of each prompt's content
tips: Use 'Select All' and 'Select None' for quick bulk selection
You can import just some prompts from a folder — untick the folder and tick individual prompts

import_dialog_destination.title: Destination Folders
import_dialog_destination.description:
Choose where the imported prompts should be placed in your library.

You can tick MULTIPLE folders — each imported prompt will be placed in every folder you select. This is useful if you want a prompt available in more than one workspace.

[+] marks top-level folders, |- marks subfolders nested inside them.

If 'Recreate original folder structure' is ticked (for files exported from folders), the original folder hierarchy will be recreated and the destination checkboxes are ignored.
tips: Tick multiple folders to place prompts in several locations at once
If no destination is selected, you'll be prompted to choose one
New folders will be created automatically if the original folder names don't exist in your library

import_dialog_preview.title: Prompt Preview
import_dialog_preview.description:
Shows the full text of a prompt before you decide to import it.

To preview a prompt:

- Right-click any prompt in the items list above, or
- Tick a prompt and press Ctrl+P

The preview expands to show the prompt name, version count and full text.
tips: You can preview different prompts without changing your tick selections
The preview shows the current (latest) version of the prompt

import_dialog_duplicates.title: Duplicate Handling
import_dialog_duplicates.description:
Controls what happens when an imported prompt has the same name as one that already exists in the destination folder:

• Skip duplicate: the imported prompt is ignored and your existing one is kept unchanged.
• Rename (add number): the imported prompt is added with a number appended, e.g. 'My Prompt (2)'.
• Overwrite: your existing prompt is replaced with the imported version.
tips: 'Rename' is the safest option — you keep both versions
'Overwrite' is useful when you're updating prompts from a colleague
'Skip' is best when you only want genuinely new prompts

thread_delete_branch_button.title: 🗑 Delete Branch
thread_delete_branch_button.description:
Permanently delete the currently displayed conversation branch.

The source document is not affected — only this conversation thread is deleted. The action cannot be undone.

If other branches exist for the same source document, the viewer will switch to one of them automatically after deletion.
tips: Source documents cannot be deleted from this button — use Documents Library for that
If this is the only branch for a source document you will be warned before deleting

thread_delete_exchange_button.title: 🗑 Delete Exchange
thread_delete_exchange_button.description:
Delete the exchange (question + answer pair) where your cursor is currently positioned.

Click inside an exchange first to position your cursor there, then click this button. The exchange is removed from the conversation thread.

This cannot be undone.
tips: Click inside the exchange you want to delete before pressing this button
Use with care — the deletion cannot be reversed

transcript_speaker_filter.title: 🎤 Show Speaker
transcript_speaker_filter.description:
Filter the transcript to show only paragraphs from a selected speaker.

Choose a speaker name from the dropdown to hide all other speakers' paragraphs, making it easier to review or edit a single speaker's contribution.

Select 'All speakers' to restore the full transcript view.

This filter is only shown when the transcript contains two or more distinct speaker labels.
tips: Filtering does not delete paragraphs — all speakers are still there when you switch back to 'All speakers'
Useful for checking the accuracy of speaker assignments before finalising

transcript_identify_button.title: 🏷 Identify Speakers
transcript_identify_button.description:
Open the speaker identification panel to assign real names to the speakers in this transcript.

This button only appears when the transcript contains unresolved heuristic speaker labels (shown as SPEAKER_A, SPEAKER_B, etc.).

The identification process runs in two steps:
1. A dialog asks you to name each speaker found
2. A floating panel lets you click through paragraphs and confirm or correct each assignment

You can also use 'Identify all' in the panel to bulk-assign all paragraphs at once using the heuristic labels as a guide.
tips: Click any paragraph in the transcript while the panel is open to load it into the panel
Use the Pause button in the panel if you want to edit the transcript mid-session
Speaker assignments are saved automatically when you close the panel

transcript_edit_button.title: ✏ Edit Transcript  /  💾 Save Edits
transcript_edit_button.description:
Toggle transcript editing mode on or off.

Click '✏ Edit' to enter edit mode — the transcript becomes editable so you can correct words, fix punctuation, or make other adjustments. The Split and Merge buttons become active.

Click '💾 Save' (the same button changes label) to save your edits and return to read-only mode. Edits are saved to the document library.

Use ↩ Undo to step back through recent changes if you make a mistake.
tips: Only word-level corrections are recommended here — use Split and Merge for structural changes
Press Enter while editing to split the paragraph at the cursor position
Edits are saved to the document library when you click Save

transcript_audio_links_button.title: 🔗 Audio Links
transcript_audio_links_button.description:
Toggle whether clicking a paragraph plays the audio from that point in the recording.

When Audio Links are active (button appears green / pressed in), clicking any paragraph in the transcript seeks the audio player to the timestamp for that paragraph and starts playback.

When inactive, clicking paragraphs does not affect audio playback.
tips: Audio links require the original audio file to still be present on your computer
The audio player controls remain visible regardless of this setting

transcript_undo_button.title: ↩ Undo
transcript_undo_button.description:
Undo the last structural change to the transcript — a paragraph split, merge, or speaker rename.

This undo applies to structural edits only (Split, Merge, and speaker label changes). It does not undo word-level corrections made in edit mode — use Ctrl+Z for those while editing.

Up to 3 levels of undo are available. The button is disabled when there is nothing to undo.
tips: This undo is for structural changes (Split / Merge / Rename speaker) only
For undoing word corrections while in edit mode, use Ctrl+Z instead
Up to 3 previous states are stored

transcript_split_button.title: ✂ Split Paragraph
transcript_split_button.description:
Split the paragraph at the cursor position into two separate paragraphs.

Place your cursor where you want to split, then click this button (or press Enter while in edit mode). The split happens at the nearest sentence boundary to keep both halves grammatically clean.

The split preview label above this button shows exactly where the split will occur before you commit.

Only available while Edit mode is active.
tips: The split preview label shows a preview of the break point before you click
Splits happen at the nearest sentence-ending punctuation (. ? !)
Use ↩ Undo if the result is not what you expected

transcript_merge_button.title: ⊕ Merge with Next
transcript_merge_button.description:
Merge the paragraph at the cursor position with the paragraph immediately below it.

Place your cursor anywhere within the paragraph you want to merge, then click this button. The two paragraphs are joined into one, with leading and trailing whitespace cleaned up.

Only available while Edit mode is active.
tips: The merged paragraph takes the speaker label of the first (upper) paragraph
Use ↩ Undo if you merge paragraphs by mistake

_elevator_pitch.title: Why Use DocAnalyser?
_elevator_pitch.content:
Why Use DocAnalyser?
What it does, who it's for, and when to use it over a standard AI web interface.

If you already use ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini through a web browser,
you know how powerful AI can be for working with text. DocAnalyser is
built around a different idea: that serious document work needs more
than a conversation interface. It needs a workflow.

## 🗂 A Library, Not Just a Chat History

Web AI interfaces save your conversations — but even the best of them
store documents and analyses in the same flat list of chats or project
folders, with no structural connection between a source document and
the analysis it produced.

DocAnalyser organises everything around the document. Each source lives
in a searchable library alongside every summary, analysis, and
conversation thread it has generated. Load a document once. Return to
it six months later. Search for it by title or content. Pick up the
conversation exactly where you left it — or run an entirely different
prompt against the same source, using a different AI model, with one
click.

## 📄 No More Copy-Paste for the Sources That Matter Most

Web AI interfaces let you upload common documents — but there are
categories of content where DocAnalyser does the work for you that
web interfaces simply cannot:

  🎬  Paste a YouTube URL          →  transcript fetched automatically
  📻  Paste a Substack/podcast URL →  article and/or audio fetched
  📰  Any Apple Podcasts URL        →  episode audio transcribed
  🌐  Paste a web article URL      →  article text extracted

Everything that is loaded is automatically saved to the Documents
Library — not lost when the browser tab closes.

## 📏 Structured Processing for Long Documents

All AI models have a limit on how much text they can process in a
single exchange. While the largest modern models can accept very long
documents, DocAnalyser's chunked processing provides something
different: reliable, structured analysis that works consistently across
all providers and all model sizes — without you needing to think about
limits at all.

For large documents — a full interview archive, a book-length report,
a batch of meeting transcripts — DocAnalyser splits the material into
manageable sections, processes each one, and assembles a coherent
result automatically. The same workflow works whether you're using the
most powerful cloud model or a small one running locally on your own
computer.

## 🎙 Audio and Video — An Integrated Workflow

Some web AI interfaces can now accept uploaded audio files and return
a transcript. That's a starting point — but it's a long way from what
DocAnalyser provides.

When DocAnalyser transcribes a recording, the result is a structured
document, not a block of chat text:

  ✔  Timestamps for every paragraph
  ✔  Speaker identification — who said what
  ✔  Click any paragraph to play the audio from that point
  ✔  Audio-linked summaries with "jump to" seek links
  ✔  Transcript saved to your library alongside all AI analyses
  ✔  Local transcription option — audio never leaves your computer

The audio-linked summary feature is particularly distinctive: ask
DocAnalyser's AI to summarise a two-hour interview, and each key point
in the summary appears as a clickable link that seeks the audio player
to the exact moment where that point was made. No web AI interface
offers this.

For researchers working with sensitive community recordings, the local
transcription option — which processes everything on your own computer
— provides a level of privacy that cloud-only interfaces cannot match.

## 📎 Analyse Multiple Documents Together

Standard AI interfaces are optimised for conversation, not research.
While some now offer project workspaces with file uploads, DocAnalyser
is designed from the ground up for research — where the real insight
often comes from working across many sources simultaneously, drawing
on documents that were loaded weeks or months apart.

Attach multiple documents from your library to a single prompt:

  "Summarise these six interviews into common themes"
  "Compare the approach taken in each of these three reports"
  "What does this new article add to what I've already read?"
  "What do all of these agree on — and where do they differ?"

## 📋 Your Prompt Library — and Share It With Others

If you find yourself typing the same instructions to an AI over and
over — "summarise in 200 words using plain language, structured as
key points..." — DocAnalyser's Prompt Library stores your frequently
used prompts so you can apply any of them to any document in two
clicks.

Prompts can be organised into folders, exported as a portable package,
and shared with colleagues. A research team can maintain a shared
collection of analysis prompts and import them into their own
DocAnalyser installations. No web AI interface offers this.

## 🔄 Import, Export, and Share

DocAnalyser is designed for collaborative and ongoing work, not just
one-off analysis. You can:

  • Export documents from your library to share source material or
    analysis with colleagues
  • Export your Prompt Library — or individual folders of prompts —
    as portable .docanalyser packages that others can import
  • Import document sets and prompt collections from colleagues,
    building on shared work rather than starting from scratch

This makes DocAnalyser practical for teams, research groups, and
organisations with shared analytical workflows — not just individuals.

## 🔄 Follow-up, Branching, and Picking Up Where You Left Off

Like web AI interfaces, DocAnalyser supports follow-up questions —
the AI remembers the conversation. But it goes further:

  Saved threads:  Your entire conversation is stored alongside the
                  document. Come back to it tomorrow.

  Branches:       Explore two different lines of questioning about the
                  same document without overwriting the first.

## 🔌 Choose Your AI — and Switch Freely

DocAnalyser connects to all the major AI providers:

  OpenAI (ChatGPT)  ·  Anthropic (Claude)  ·  Google (Gemini)
  xAI (Grok)  ·  DeepSeek  ·  Ollama (local models, free)

Switch providers with a single dropdown — your document and prompt
stay exactly as they are. Use the best model for each task. Built-in
cost tracking shows what you're spending across all providers.

## 🔒 Complete Privacy When You Need It

If you work with sensitive material — community stories, unpublished
research, personal records, or confidential documents — you may not
want your content passing through any external server.

DocAnalyser's local AI option (Ollama) runs an AI model on your own
computer. Its local transcription option (Faster-Whisper) processes
audio on your own computer too. Nothing leaves your machine. See the
Local AI Guide and Audio Transcription Guide (both in the Help menu)
for setup guidance.

## 💡 When to Use DocAnalyser — and When Not To

Use DocAnalyser when:

  ✔  Your source is a YouTube video, podcast, or Substack article
  ✔  Your source is an audio recording that needs timestamps,
     speaker identification, and seek links — not just a transcript
  ✔  You plan to analyse this document more than once
  ✔  You need to compare or synthesise multiple documents together
  ✔  You want to reuse the same prompts across different documents
  ✔  You want to share prompts or documents with colleagues
  ✔  Privacy matters — you'd rather not send content to the cloud
  ✔  You want to save, export, or return to your AI analyses later

Use a web AI interface when:

  →  The task is quick and one-off
  →  You just want to ask a general question (no document involved)
  →  You don't need to save or revisit the work

Press F1 over any button or field for context-sensitive help.
Use the App Overview (Help menu) for a full feature reference.
